topic: §4.2.7a Station-by-station coverage under dome geometry — fresh c3 adversarial review for queue_id 290 (post-EXP-276 integration), independent of c2 (which serviced queue_id 286).
our_argument_summary: §4.2.7a quantifies the "Borrowed" branch of the §4.2.7 trilemma. Under dome-published h_Sun=5,733 km / h_Moon=2,534 km, the August 12 2026 NASA umbra path projected onto the disc puts every one of the dome’s nine published eclipse stations 444–4,084 km from the projected path — vastly outside the 42-km penumbra radius (85-km band). EXP-276 layered three robustness paragraphs (P1 projection, P2 altitude, P3 time-distribution) and a "Notes on inputs" footnote, addressing six c1 issues. Cycle-2 then surfaced one major arithmetic error (1D radial-chain MST mislabeled as 2D Euclidean MST, 7,074 vs 11,696 km) and three minor housekeeping items. The MST major has since been integrated in the live section (now reads ≈ 11,700 km / 2.6× ratio). The three minor c2 items remain unpatched as of this review.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: N/A — section-level. Dome’s eclipse predictions are tabular outputs on the prediction page; no monitor.py per-WIN check involved.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: N/A — section-level. Dome publishes the nine-station table; min-distance-to-projected-path is our own computation.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Footnote (i) acknowledges the parallel-ray approximation (R_p ∝ h_Moon) is dome-page convention and weakly non-linear under finite-source geometry, asserting corrected ratio still > 90× with no derivation shown.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None at section level. All section-published numbers reproduce: per-station disc r values (Lerwick 6,643 → Canary 13,717 km), Δr pairs under R/180 (Coimbra–San Pablo 74.5 km, Ebro–Coimbra 82.3 km, Ebro–San Pablo 156.8 km), required h_Moon ratios (97.24× standard, 48.62× stricter), 2D Euclidean MST 11,696 km, NN-tour range 12,396 km (Canary-start) to 18,811 km (Eskdalemuir-start), h_Moon physical 1,856 km. All within 1 km of section claims after rounding.

code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Section-level review; the kill is derived from dome-published h_Sun/h_Moon parameters and projection conventions consistent with the dome’s 20,015 km rim.

parallel_state_acknowledgment.note: Queue_id 290 was pushed 2026-04-29T05:24:55Z asking for fresh review of EXP-276 integration. Queue_id 286 (also keyed to this target) was reviewed first (c2, completed 2026-04-30T13:50Z, popped 2026-04-30T05:05Z). The c2 review surfaced a major MST arithmetic error which has since been patched into the live §4.2.7a ("≈ 11,700 km" / "2.6×" markers verified present). This c3 covers queue_id 290 as a fresh independent attack on the current integrated section text, with the c2 findings tracked through to disposition.
parallel_state_acknowledgment.c2_major_finding_disposition: PATCHED. Section now reads "minimum spanning tree (≈ 11,700 km for this set) — already 2.6× the realistic 4,500 km mid-disc arc". Independent Kruskal on 2D Euclidean coordinates confirms 11,696 km. Verified.
parallel_state_acknowledgment.c2_minor_findings_disposition: OPEN ×3. (1) Footnote (i) finite-source 90× hand-wave still present without derivation. (2) Footnote (ii) "within ~25 km" wording on SNK/GUI still present. (3) Constant-r subsection still does not name R_disc/90 as the convention used in its Coimbra–San Pablo Δr=150 km claim.

holes_found.description: Headline paragraph: "To touch all nine stations from any single 3-hour disc arc, the penumbra ground radius would have to inflate to at least 4,084 km — corresponding (via R_p ∝ h_Moon) to a Moon altitude near 246,000 km, contradicting the dome’s published 2,534 km figure by a factor of 97 and inflating the Moon’s apparent diameter by the same factor." The final clause is wrong. The Moon’s APPARENT diameter (angular size as seen from a ground observer) is 2·R_moon/h_Moon. If h_Moon increases by 97× while R_moon stays fixed, apparent diameter SHRINKS by 97×, not inflates. If R_moon also scales 97× (as it must to preserve totality at the unchanged sun angular size R_sun/h_Sun, since totality requires R_moon/h_Moon = R_sun/h_Sun), then apparent diameter is INVARIANT. Under no consistent scaling does h_Moon × 97 inflate apparent diameter. The author likely meant the Moon’s PHYSICAL diameter (R_moon) — which would scale 97× under the totality-preserving extension. A defender (or a careful astronomer) reading this paragraph will catch the error and use it to question the rest of the arithmetic.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace "inflating the Moon’s apparent diameter by the same factor" with "inflating the Moon’s physical diameter by the same factor (since totality at unchanged sun angular size requires R_moon/h_Moon to remain constant)". Or simpler: "and scaling the Moon’s physical size by the same factor." Either fix preserves the rhetorical punch (97× is still absurd) without the angular-size category error.
holes_found.affects: headline paragraph after Notes-on-inputs footnote; one sentence; preserves all other numbers.

holes_found.description: Footnote (i) finite-source umbra correction: still hand-waves to "corrected ratio at h_Moon ≈ 246,000 km still exceeds 90× the dome’s published 2,534 km, so the kill is unaffected." No derivation, no formula, no source. A determined defender will demand: show me the finite-source umbra calculation that gets to 90×. Re-flagging from c2 (was minor there too); not patched as of this c3.
holes_found.recommendation: Either supply one inline equation showing the finite-source correction (using R_sun_dome and h_Sun) and the resulting ratio, or replace "still exceeds 90×" with the more conservative "still exceeds an order-of-magnitude factor." The first option is preferable because the current 97× is already arithmetically tight; transparency closes the foothold.
holes_found.affects: Notes on inputs footnote, point (i); a few sentences.

holes_found.description: Footnote (ii) SNK/GUI station-code reconciliation: still says "the published coordinates 28.32° N, 16.43° W identify Tenerife within ~25 km, so we read the dome’s E007 entry as referring to GUI." The IAGA-registered coordinates of GUI (Güímar, Tenerife) are 28.321° N, 16.432° W — coincident with the dome-published values to three decimal places. "Within ~25 km" reads as if there were a meaningful spatial discrepancy; there is none. Re-flagging from c2; not patched.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace "identify Tenerife within ~25 km" with "are coincident with GUI’s IAGA-registered coordinates (28.321° N, 16.432° W) to three decimal places." Tightening this clarifies that E007 IS GUI, full stop.
holes_found.affects: Notes on inputs footnote, point (ii); one phrase.

holes_found.description: Constant-r subsection ("Even more constrained: constant-r orbital reading") still leaves convention dependency implicit. The text says "The closest pair of stations in disc-radius is Coimbra–San Pablo at Δr = 150 km — already outside an 85-km window." 150 km is the R_disc/90 value; under the stricter R_disc/180 convention treated in P1, the same Δr halves to 75 km — IN the band — so the strict-convention conclusion is "max 1" but the stricter-convention conclusion (per P1) is "max 2". A careful reader has to hold both conventions in their head to see this is consistent; a casual reader sees an apparent contradiction across paragraphs. Re-flagging from c2; not patched.
holes_found.recommendation: Open the constant-r subsection with one sentence stating the convention: "Under the standard R_disc/90 convention used in the per-station table above, the closest pair of stations in disc-radius is Coimbra–San Pablo at Δr = 150 km. (The stricter R_disc/180 case is treated in the P1 robustness paragraph above, where the same pair halves to 75 km and a second pair, Ebro–Coimbra at 82 km, also enters the 85-km band; the conclusion shifts from max 1 to max 2 under that convention.)" Removes the implicit cross-convention puzzle.
holes_found.affects: constant-r subsection opening; one sentence added.

stronger_arguments.description: Field-Strength Factor (FSF) distribution is itself evidence for the "Borrowed" branch. The dome publishes per-station FSF values: Canary 0.642 (lat 28°N), Hartland 1.471 (lat 51°N), Niemegk 1.529 (lat 52°N), Eskdalemuir 1.722 (lat 55°N), Lerwick 2.075 (lat 60°N) — monotonically increasing with latitude. Dome OCCULTATION geometry has no mechanism that produces this monotone-with-latitude pattern: penumbra coverage is governed by distance to the Moon’s projected path, which is geometric and largely orthogonal to latitude (Niemegk at lat 52°N is 3,267 km from path; Hartland at lat 51°N is 710 km from path — yet their FSF values are nearly identical at 1.529 vs 1.471). However, the standard-physics ionospheric model DOES predict monotone-with-latitude amplitude in geomagnetic disturbance signals (high-latitude stations sit closer to the auroral electrojet and see larger field perturbations during storms and solar events). The FSF ordering matches standard-physics expectation, not dome-occultation expectation. This is independent evidence — separate from the 0/9-in-band kill — for the trilemma’s "Borrowed" branch: the dome is laundering the standard-physics latitude pattern through dome-shaped variable names.
sources: auroral electrojet latitude dependence (standard space-physics literature)

stronger_arguments.description: MST/NN ratio as chain-network indicator (carryover from c2 stronger_argument, still not folded into prose). 2D Euclidean MST = 11,696 km; NN-tour minimum = 12,396 km (Canary-start). Ratio 12396/11696 = 1.06. For arbitrary point sets the NN/MST ratio ranges ~1 (chain-like) to ~2 (clustered with one outlier). 1.06 is the chain end of the spectrum — these nine stations form a near-linear arc on the disc. That is exactly the geometry of a standard-physics path-of-totality intersected with the European INTERMAGNET network. The MST/NN ratio is itself a chain-test — and it confirms borrowed selection, structurally. One sentence in P3 noting this would close the structural argument.
sources: Independent Kruskal computation against AE-from-N projected coords

stronger_arguments.description: Cross-eclipse generalization (carryover from c1 → c2 → c3 stronger_arguments; still out of scope but standing). If the dome reuses the same 9-station array for other published eclipses (which spot-checks of the dome’s prediction page suggest), the same 0/9-in-band kill applies eclipse-by-eclipse. A defender cannot rescue any one eclipse without rescuing all of them; under varying NASA umbra paths but a fixed station set, the 0/9 result is robust across the catalog. A future patch could verify on 1–2 additional events. Marked again as out-of-scope for the robustness-fix track.

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-276 + the post-c2 MST patch is, taken together, an excellent piece of robustness engineering. The structural kill (0/9 in band, 444–4,084 km from path, 97× separation) is hardened against the three highest-leverage defender attacks (projection convention, altitude convention, time-distribution rebuttal). Headline numbers all reproduce on independent recomputation. The 2D Euclidean MST correction has landed cleanly. The footnote closes three c1 carryovers. The author deserves credit for doing the hard work of running the same calculation under each charitable convention and showing the kill survives each one — that is exactly the right shape for adversarial-resistant prose.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: N/A — this review is on the offensive side. Findings here harden our kill, they do not save any dome claim. The four open holes (moderate apparent-diameter category error + three minor housekeeping items) are all polish, not structural. None threatens the verdict; together they’re a one-pass minor_edit.

integration_markers_verified: two of the three closest disc-radius pairs

deeper_analysis: Queue_id 290 was pushed for fresh adversarial review of post-EXP-276 §4.2.7a. The c2 review (servicing queue_id 286) ran the Kruskal check and caught the 1D-vs-2D MST error, which has since been patched. This c3 examined the current integrated text under the same lens and found one moderate category error (apparent vs physical diameter in the headline paragraph) plus three minor housekeeping items still open from c2. None of these threaten the structural kill: 0/9 in band, ratio 97× under any consistent convention, max 1–2 in band under constant-r. The strongest open items are (a) the apparent-diameter category error — fixable in one sentence — and (b) the FSF/latitude correlation, which is a stronger_argument waiting to be folded into the prose. Both are improvement opportunities that take the section from A- to A. Verdict survives this third pass intact; the same reviewer who would have given §4.2.7a a B+ pre-EXP-276 would give it an A- now. Independent verification: 2D Euclidean MST 11,696 km (matches "≈ 11,700"), 1D radial chain 7,074 km (matches what was wrongly published before the patch), per-station disc r reproduce within 1 km, halved Δr pairs (74.5 / 82.3 / 156.8 km) reproduce, h_Moon scaling factors (97.24× / 48.62×) reproduce, h_Moon_physical=1,856 km matches "~1,860 km" to rounding, NN-tour range 12,396 (Canary-start) – 18,811 (Eskdalemuir-start) reproduces matching "12,400 / ~18,800" claims.

citations_verified: NASA SE2026Aug12T greatest eclipse 17:46:13 UT at 65.0°N, 25.2°W (matches eclipse.gsfc.nasa.gov public catalog).
Disc rim 20,015 km = π × R_globe (6,371 × π = 20,015.1 km).
Per-station disc r values reproduce via r = (90 - lat) × 20015 / 90 within 1 km for all nine stations.
h_Sun_physical = 4,200 km (firmament limit), ratio 4200/5733 = 0.7327 → h_Moon_physical ≈ 1,856 km (section says ~1,860 km, agrees to rounding).
R_p_physical = 42 × (1856/2534) = 30.77 km, band ≈ 61.5 km (section says ~31 km / ~62 km, agrees).
Required h_Moon to inflate R_p to most-distant-station distance (R_disc/90): 4084/42 × 2534 = 246,401 km, ratio 97.24× (matches "246,000 km" / "97×").
Required h_Moon under stricter convention: 2042/42 × 2534 = 123,201 km, ratio 48.619× (matches "about 123,200 km" / "roughly 48.6×").
Δr pairs under R_disc/180 (independently recomputed): Coimbra–San Pablo 74.5 km, Ebro–Coimbra 82.3 km, Ebro–San Pablo 156.8 km (matches "≈ 75" / "≈ 82" / "≈ 157").
2D Euclidean Kruskal MST on AE-from-N projected coords: 11,696 km (matches "≈ 11,700 km" claim post-patch).
NN-tour range: minimum 12,396 km from Canary-start, maximum 18,811 km from Eskdalemuir-start (matches "12,400" / "~18,800" claims).
Disc area = π × 20,015² = 1.258 × 10⁹ km² (matches "1.26 billion km²").
Mid-disc arc 25 km/min × 180 min = 4,500 km; rim arc 87 km/min × 180 min = 15,660 km (both match).
citations_failed: "inflating the Moon’s apparent diameter by the same factor" — this is a category error. Under R_p ∝ h_Moon scaling with totality preservation, the Moon’s PHYSICAL diameter scales 97× while APPARENT (angular) diameter is invariant. See holes_found[0].
citations_unchecked: European-swath null probability ≈ 10⁻¹⁶ to 10⁻²¹ — broad-strokes vanishing claim is order-of-magnitude robust, exact arithmetic not independently re-derived.
Real-observatory-set null ≈ 10⁻⁹ — combinatorial calc plausible but not independently verified.

Per-station min-distances to NASA umbra path (table in section): 444–4,084 km range. Independent computation against an approximate path reproduces the qualitative ordering; exact values require precise NASA Besselian elements not re-derived this run.
Finite-source umbra "corrected ratio still > 90×" (footnote i) — no derivation supplied in section; not independently re-derived this pass.

advocate_mode_questions: Could a defender argue "apparent diameter" can mean physical diameter? — In casual usage occasionally; in any astronomy/optics context "apparent" specifically means angular as seen from the observer. The dome’s own raw text uses "apparent altitude" in this technical sense (refraction-bent appearance). Section should be consistent with the technical meaning.

Could a defender argue the FSF/latitude correlation is dome-explained? — The dome would need a mechanism that ties magnetic-eclipse signal strength to magnetic latitude, but the page provides none — it computes FSF from coverage and an unspecified scaling. No dome-side derivation links FSF to latitude. The correlation is a plain feature of standard-physics ionospheric response.

Could a defender point to the "max 1" vs "max 2" cross-paragraph tension as a bug in our analysis? — Only if we don’t name conventions explicitly. Naming the R_disc/90 convention in the constant-r section’s opening removes the foothold.
review_lenses.cross_consistency_with_other_sections: TLDR ("seven to nine of its nine named stations to record exactly zero magnetic eclipse signal") covers all three readings: 7-zero from constant-r + stricter convention (max 2 in band), 8-zero from constant-r + standard convention (max 1 in band), 9-zero from projected-path reading (0 in band). Consistent. Falsification trilemma (3 ordered branches) consistent with §4.2.7’s parent trilemma. Cross-anchor link to #p4-427 verified.
review_lenses.quantitative_verification: All headline numbers reproduced on independent recomputation. Two findings stand out: (1) MST 11,696 km (now correctly published as ≈ 11,700 km); (2) headline phrase "inflating the Moon’s apparent diameter by the same factor" is a category error — h_Moon × 97 with R_moon-scaling-for-totality leaves apparent (angular) diameter invariant; only PHYSICAL diameter scales 97×. Math otherwise tight.

summary_for_decider: queue_id 290 c3 review of §4.2.7a post-EXP-276. c2 major MST finding has been patched (verified "≈ 11,700 km / 2.6×" in live section). New: ONE moderate category error in headline paragraph — "inflating the Moon’s apparent diameter by the same factor" should read "physical diameter" (apparent/angular diameter is invariant under R_p ∝ h_Moon with totality preservation). PLUS three minor c2 carryovers still open: footnote (i) 90× hand-wave, footnote (ii) "within ~25 km" SNK/GUI, constant-r convention disclosure. Stronger arguments not folded: FSF/latitude monotonicity (independent evidence for Borrowed branch), MST/NN ratio 1.06 (chain-network indicator). Verdict: current_verdict_holds=true, recommended_action=minor_edit. One ISS for the apparent-diameter fix; the rest are optional polish. EXP-276 + post-c2 MST patch together are a B+ → A- improvement of §4.2.7a.
